summary: Small cleanup and matching-oriented type/header adjustments across item code, with one fighter comment addition. The most substantive changes moved itMBallAttributes from itCharItems.h to itCommonItems.h, removed an unnecessary itCharItems.h include from itmball.c, gave explicit array lengths to ItemLogicTable externs, adjusted style/null constants in it_2E6A.c, and preserved pointer-to-pointer declarations for item article globals after review clarified that Article** and Article*[] are not ABI-equivalent for sbss/bss placement.
what_changed: Added a brief documentation comment to ftLib_80086EB4 identifying it as getting a fighter's team ID.
Reformatted comments and alignment in itFoxBlaster_ItemVars without apparent semantic changes.
Moved the itMBallAttributes struct definition out of itCharItems.h and into itCommonItems.h, matching its use by the common item mball code.
Removed the now-unneeded include of it/itCharItems.h from itmball.c, leaving it to include itCommonItems.h for itMBallAttributes.
Reordered/normalized forward includes in itCommonItems.h and cleaned indentation of the anonymous union inside it_2E6A_ItemVars_1.
Changed ItemLogicTable extern declarations in it_2725.h from unsized arrays to explicit sizes: it_803F23CC[47] and it_803F4D20[30].

In it_2E6A.c, replaced a literal zero argument to lb_8000B1CC with NULL, replaced spawn.kind - 0x2B with spawn.kind - It_Kind_Kuriboh, and applied line-wrapping/style changes to callbacks and infinite loop formatting.
Added comments around it_804D6D40 and the #if 0 it_8027870C stub noting uncertainty about type/ItCo.dat loading.
smart_moves: Used a named item kind constant, It_Kind_Kuriboh, instead of the magic value 0x2B when indexing it_804D6D38, making the table offset self-documenting while preserving the same computation.
Kept itMBallAttributes in a common item header rather than a character-item header, reducing unnecessary coupling and allowing itmball.c to drop itCharItems.h.
Added explicit array extents for ItemLogicTable globals where sizes were known, which improves type information for later decompilation without changing data definitions.
Accepted/retained pointer-to-pointer declarations for article table globals instead of converting them to arrays after review explained the ABI/storage-section consequence.
decomp_lessons: Do not assume T** and T*[] are interchangeable in decomp declarations. An Article** can be a single pointer in sbss, while Article*[] declares the pointed-to region itself and may force ordinary bss placement; this can break matches even if source-level indexing looks similar.
When globals are in small data/sbss, preserving pointer-vs-array spelling can matter for ABI and section layout, not just C type readability.
Prefer named enum/constants over raw item-kind offsets when the generated code remains equivalent; it makes future table-index reasoning easier.

Move small structs to the narrowest shared header that matches actual consumers. Avoid including broad character item headers from common item implementation files when a common item header is sufficient.
Explicit extern array sizes are useful documentation and type information, but should be introduced only when the table length is evidenced.
naming_conventions: Kept existing placeholder-style item variable names such as it_2E6A_ItemVars_1, xDD4_itemVar, x4_vec, and it_804D6D40 where exact semantics were still uncertain.
Used project enum-style item kind naming, e.g. It_Kind_Kuriboh, to replace a magic numeric kind base.
Preserved existing decomp symbol-address naming for globals and tables such as it_803F23CC, it_803F4D20, it_804D6D38, and it_804D6D40.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use NULL rather than 0 for pointer arguments when it clarifies intent and does not disturb matching.
Be cautious changing extern global declarations from pointer-to-pointer to array form; such changes can alter sbss versus bss placement and cause match failures.
Explicit array bounds on extern declarations can improve correctness/readability while keeping references compatible when the bound matches known table data.
Replacing magic constants with equivalent enum constants can improve readability while preserving arithmetic and generated code, as shown by spawn.kind - It_Kind_Kuriboh.

review_feedback.author: AaronB-Hub
review_feedback.feedback: Noted that the build/matching complained when it_804D6D24, it_804D6D30, and it_804D6D38 were changed from Article** to Article*[], despite a similar-looking it_804A0F60 case not complaining.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Explained that Article*[] is a contiguous region of Article* pointers, whereas Article** is a pointer to such a region. Article** can be stored in sbss, while Article*[] may be large enough for ordinary bss, so the types are not ABI-equivalent and can break matches.

follow_up_queries: Find other PRs or files where Article** globals were changed to Article*[] and check for sbss/bss match regressions.
Audit item globals near it_804D6D24, it_804D6D30, it_804D6D38, and it_804D6D40 for pointer-vs-array declarations before attempting type cleanup.
Search for common item structs still declared in character item headers and consider moving them to common headers only when include ownership is clear.
Verify the evidence for ItemLogicTable sizes it_803F23CC[47] and it_803F4D20[30] against data tables before reusing those lengths elsewhere.
Investigate the noted ItCo.dat/usd loading stub around it_8027870C and the uncertain type of it_804D6D40.
